summary:
Erythromycin can cause significant QT interval prolongation leading to potential fatal heart rhythm issues; its interaction with terfenadine likely caused this adverse event for the patient suffering from both conditions simultaneously. To prevent future complications, alternative antibiotics should be considered if similar treatment needs arise again. ```plaintext
Erythromycin causes QT interval prolongation which may lead to serious cardiac arrhythmias, particularly when used concomitantly with terfenadine as seen in this case. This drug combination explains the development of cardiac symptoms noted in the patient.
```
